items.title: Researcher breaks 15-bit elliptic curve key in ‘largest quantum attack,’ wins 1 bitcoin bounty from Project Eleven
items.desc: Independent researcher Giancarlo Lelli derived a 15-bit elliptic curve key on a publicly accessible quantum computer, Project Eleven said.

items.title: Bitcoin’s Quantum Problem Is Really a Governance Crisis in Disguise: UTXO
items.desc: Bitcoin Magazine Bitcoin’s Quantum Problem Is Really a Governance Crisis in Disguise: UTXO Guillaume Girard of UTXO Management argues that while a quantum computer capable of breaking Bitcoin may never arrive, the network must prepare now because protocol changes move slowly, like a state legislature. This post Bitcoin’s Quantum Problem Is Really a Governance Crisis in Disguise: UTXO first appeared on Bitcoin Magazine and is written by Micah Zimmerman.

items.title: The $145 billion math: Why bitcoin’s quantum threat is manageable, not existential
items.desc: Quantum fears focus on vulnerable early wallets, but market data suggests even a worst case sell-off would be large, not catastrophic.

items.title: Zondacrypto CEO goes off radar as Poland probe deepens
items.desc: Polish prosecutors are investigating Zondacrypto over alleged fraud and fund access issues, while local media report that CEO Przemysław Kral is in Israel.

items.title: Fluent launches Ethereum Layer 2 mainnet and BLEND token with $50 million day-one liquidity
items.desc: Fluent launched its Ethereum Layer 2 mainnet alongside its BLEND token and a native stablecoin with $50 million in day-one liquidity.

items.title: Morgan Stanley launches stablecoin reserves fund aligned with GENIUS Act requirements
items.desc: Morgan Stanley launched a government money market fund for stablecoin issuers to hold reserves in line with GENIUS Act requirements.

items.title: Metaplanet raises $50M in zero-interest bonds to buy more Bitcoin
items.desc: Metaplanet raises $50 million through zero-interest bonds from EVO FUND to expand its Bitcoin treasury, extending its capital markets-driven accumulation strategy.

items.title: Aave Leads ‘DeFi United’ Push to Contain $292M KelpDAO Fallout
items.desc: Aave's founder has pledged 5,000 ETH of his own money as a growing DeFi coalition races to contain the fallout from the KelpDAO exploit.

items.title: Bitcoin, dollar move in near-perfect opposition. It hasn't been this extreme in almost 4 years.
items.desc: What you need to know for April 24, 2026

items.title: Bitcoin stalls below at $77,500 as volatility cools, traders unwind leverage
items.desc: BTC holds a tight range as open interest drops, signaling cooling momentum, while altcoins show mixed performance and ZEC attracts fresh bullish interest.

items.title: Michael Saylor says the bitcoin winter is over. Some experts agree, with caveats.
items.desc: Market analyst Mati Greenspan said bitcoin has not gone through a “winter,” rather a pullback within a broader bull market, adding the next leg up for bitcoin will be driven by nation-state adoption.

items.title: Spot bitcoin ETFs draw $2 billion in net inflows over 8-day positive streak
items.desc: Spot bitcoin ETFs logged $223.2 million in net inflows on Thursday, led by $167.5 million into BlackRock's IBIT.

items.title: India pushes e-rupee through welfare pilots as BRICS digital currency plan takes shape
items.desc: Farmers and food programs anchor 10 pilots aimed at boosting usage, while New Delhi eyes BRICS CBDC link ahead of 2026 summit

items.title: Bitcoin-holder Metaplanet raises $50 million to buy more BTC
items.desc: Metaplanet has issued 8 billion yen, or about $50 million, in zero-interest bonds to fund additional bitcoin purchases.

items.title: Mantle proposes up to 30,000 ETH loan to address Aave bad debt from Kelp exploit
items.desc: The Mantle team wrote that the loan would generate yield and strengthen the relationship between Mantle and Aave.

items.title: Metaplanet issues $50 million in zero-interest bonds to buy more bitcoin
items.desc: In the first quarter of this year, Metaplanet purchased 5,075 BTC, bringing its total to 40,177 BTC as of March 31.

items.title: US DOJ strike force 'restrains' $701M in crypto in ongoing scam crackdown
items.desc: Besides freezing crypto, the strike force also seized a Telegram channel used to recruit unsuspecting job seekers and took down 503 fake crypto investment websites.

items.title: Morgan Stanley is positioning itself as the reserve manager for the stablecoin industry
items.desc: The Wall Street giant has announced a fund build specifically for stablecoin issuers. Here is what it does and means for the market.

items.title: Morgan Stanley launches stablecoin offering through money market fund
items.desc: Stablecoin issuers must invest a minimum of $10 million into Morgan Stanley’s money market fund, MSNXX, to access the stablecoin reserve offering.

items.title: Bitcoin ETFs just pulled in $2 billion in 8 days while short-term holders quietly started selling
items.desc: Spot bitcoin ETFs logged their first 8-day inflow streak since October, but on-chain profit-taking is already running at 3x the rate that has marked every local top this year.

items.title: Jane Street asks US court to toss Terraform’s insider trading suit
items.desc: Jane Street has filed to dismiss Terraform’s insider trading suit, arguing the reasons for Terra’s collapse have already been litigated.

items.title: Bitcoin rally is stalling as Japanese inflation adds to Iran war–driven market jitters
items.desc: Crypto markets weaken amid rising Japan inflation, Iran war oil disruptions, and expectations of a hawkish Bank of Japan.

items.title: Crypto protocols pledge 43K ETH to restore rsETH backing
items.desc: Mantle, EtherFi Foundation, Golem Foundation, Lido DAO, Ethena, LayerZero, Ink Foundation and Tyrdo have all made pledges to the “DeFi United” recovery effort.

items.title: US soldier charged over $400K Polymarket bet on Maduro’s capture
items.desc: US prosecutors alleged that Gannon Ken Van Dyke asked Polymarket to delete his account after profiting from trades tied to the military operation in Venezuela.

items.title: Critical Bitcoin trend change in works, but analysts say daily close above $80K required
items.desc: Bitcoin’s rally above $79,000 may be a sign that the downtrend is ending, but a multi-day candle close above $80,000 would help strengthen the odds of a trend change holding.

items.title: Altcoins have ‘30% to 60%’ upside if Bitcoin taps $86K: Analyst
items.desc: MN Trading Capital founder Michael van de Poppe doesn’t expect Bitcoin to drop below $75,000 in the near term, even as Polymarket traders price in a different outcome.

items.title: Seven-Day Countdown to MEGA Begins as MegaETH Clears First KPI
items.desc: The deployment of 10 Mega Mafia apps satisfies the first of three performance triggers tied to the long-awaited token launch.

items.title: US Soldier Charged for Alleged $400K Polymarket Insider Trading on Maduro Removal
items.desc: Federal prosecutors say an Army soldier made over $400K betting on Venezuela-related outcomes on Polymarket using classified intelligence.

items.title: DOJ arrests US soldier accused of using secret intel to make Polymarket bet on Nicolás Maduro’s capture
items.desc: The DOJ arrested a U.S. Army soldier accused of using confidential information to place bets on the prediction market Polymarket.

items.title: Here’s what happened in crypto today
items.desc: Need to know what happened in crypto today? Here is the latest news on daily trends and events impacting Bitcoin price, blockchain, DeFi, NFTs, Web3 and crypto regulation.

items.title: U.S. arrests Army green beret for $400,000 Polymarket bets on Venezuela raid he was in
items.desc: Master Sergeant Gannon Ken Van Dyke allegedly tried to conceal his bets about the raid on Venezuela that led to Nicolas Maduro's arrest.

items.title: Spot ETH ETF inflows hit 10-day streak: Will Ether rally to $3K next?
items.desc: Inflows to the spot Ether ETFs topped $633 million over the past 10-days, but are the flows significant enough to trigger a rally to $3,000?

items.title: Global crypto adoption slumps amid macro pressures, Turkey defies downtrend
items.desc: Retail crypto activity fell 11% in Q1 as macro pressures weighed on markets, though Turkey and emerging economies showed resilience, TRM Labs data shows.

items.title: Jane Street asks court to dismiss Terraform Labs’ insider trading lawsuit
items.desc: Terraform Labs accused Jane Street of precipitating the collapse of the UST/LUNA algorithmic stablecoin through insider trading.

items.title: Aave rallies DeFi partners to contain fallout from $292 million KelpDAO hack
items.desc: Industry players are coordinating a recovery effort as the year's biggest crypto theft rattled Aave, with Lido and EtherFi being firsts to offer aid.

items.title: Anchorage Digital adds Marinade-powered staking strategies for Solana clients
items.desc: The integration with Marinade Finance lets clients earn yield via validator selection strategies while retaining custody and control within a single platform.

items.title: Bitcoin ETF ‘flows turned positive for the year’: BNY’s global head of ETFs
items.desc: “Flows have turned positive for the year,” Global Head of ETFs at BNY Asset Servicing Ben Slavin told The Block.

items.title: Circle Economist Proposes 50% Rate Ceiling to Snap Aave's USDC Liquidity Crisis
items.desc: Some users pushed back via Aave’s governance forum, fearing the changes would fuel liquidations on the lending protocol.

items.title: Lido proposes allocating up to $5.8 million in staked ETH to back Kelp exploit shortfall
items.desc: The proposal follows last week's roughly $292-million exploit that hit Kelp DAO’s rsETH bridge,

items.title: Tether freezes $344M USDt stablecoins at US law enforcement request
items.desc: The stablecoin issuer cited "activity tied to unlawful conduct” but no further explanation for the freezing of the dollar-pegged tokens held in two wallet addresses.

items.title: Bitcoin enters disbelief phase as USDC exchange reserves push above $7.5B
items.desc: A negative Bitcoin funding rate and $7.5 billion in USDC reserves suggest traders may start positioning against the bearish trend. Will BTC price keep rising?

items.title: US Treasury Sanctions Powerful Cambodian Politician Allegedly Tied to Crypto Scam Network
items.desc: Kok An, a Cambodian senator, is accused of running a massive crypto crime operation through his many resorts and casinos.

items.title: Aave Announces 'DeFi United' Relief Fund to Restore rsETH Backing After Kelp Exploit
items.desc: Lido is the first service provider to publicly announce its participation in the relief fund via a governance proposal requesting up to 2,500 stETH.

items.title: MoonPay launches fiat-to-stablecoin virtual accounts in New York
items.desc: MoonPay has expanded its virtual accounts product to New York, allowing businesses to convert fiat into stablecoins and settle funds without prefunding across jurisdictions.

items.title: Inside the $71 million freeze on Arbitrum that has the crypto world questioning what decentralization really means
items.desc: The emergency response prevented stolen funds from moving, but sparked debate over governance, control and the limits of decentralization on Layer 2s.

items.title: 3F, built on Morpho, raises $4 million to offer leveraged exposure to tokenized assets
items.desc: 3F has raised $4 million in funding led by Maven 11, with participation from Fidelity-affiliated F-Prime, GSR, and others.

items.title: Crypto advocacy groups call action on market structure bill ‘critical‘
items.desc: More than 120 entities associated with the crypto and blockchain industry signed onto a letter urging US Senators to move forward with a crypto market structure bill.

items.title: Fold Launches 'Bitcoin Bonus' Program for Employers Following Steak 'n Shake Debut
items.desc: Publicly traded Bitcoin services firm Fold is rolling out a program to let companies grant employees bonuses in crypto.

items.title: Bitmine’s ETH staking passes 70% of holdings after latest $320 million push
items.desc: Ethereum treasury firm Bitmine has staked roughly 3.5 million ETH, worth more than $8 billion, out of its roughly 5.08 million ETH holdings.

items.title: Fold (FLD) Launches Bitcoin Bonus Program for Employers Through Fold Business Platform
items.desc: Bitcoin Magazine Fold (FLD) Launches Bitcoin Bonus Program for Employers Through Fold Business Platform Fold has launched a Bitcoin Bonus Program through its new Fold Business platform, enabling employers to offer recurring bitcoin-denominated bonuses that are automatically converted, custodied, and vested by Fold without altering existing payroll systems. This post Fold (FLD) Launches Bitcoin Bonus Program for Employers Through Fold Business Platform first appeared on Bitcoin Magazine and is written by Micah Zimmerman.

items.title: US sanctions Cambodian senator tied to crypto scam centers as DOJ restrains $700M in broader crackdown
items.desc: The US sanctioned a Cambodian senator tied to crypto scam centers as the DOJ restrained over $700 million in a broader crackdown.

items.title: MegaETH hits first KPI with 10 apps live, starts seven-day MEGA token countdown
items.desc: MegaETH tied 53.3% of its total token supply to performance-based KPIs rather than fixed time-based vesting schedules.

items.title: The market repriced DeFi in just 48 hours
items.desc: Di Bartolomeo argues that last weekend the market achieved – in real time – a notable feat that no regulator, auditor or commentator has ever done.

items.title: PUSD stablecoin deploys on ADI Chain, targeting $3T Islamic finance market
items.desc: The Shariah-compliant stablecoin backed by Gulf currencies expands to a new Layer-2 network aimed at institutional settlement in the Middle East.

items.title: Founder of Solana Token Launchpad Believe Arrested on Assault, Strangulation Charges
items.desc: Benjamin Pasternak faces criminal charges following an investor class action lawsuit and accusations of breaching an OTC token deal.

items.title: Bitcoin weekly close in focus after BTC price fails to revisit $80K
items.desc: Bitcoin brought its bull market support band as a key level for BTC price action to reclaim during the weekly candle close.

items.title: ‘Rally on trial’: Bitcoin breakout faces key $80,000 test as whales, ETF investors buy into volatility
items.desc: Analysts say bitcoin's rally still faces a stiff test from profit-taking, macro risk and a crowded resistance zone.

items.title: Bitcoin Pulls Back From $78K As Persian Gulf Risk Trumps Institutional Bid
items.desc: ETH is lagging with on-chain risk still elevated post-Kelp, while SOL dropped 3% and the broader market cap slipped 1.6% on the day.

items.title: Spain seizes crypto cold wallets in illegal manga piracy raid
items.desc: Spanish police seized crypto cold wallets tied to an alleged illegal manga site, as questions remain over whether officers can access the funds.

items.title: French Weather Agency Alerts Police to Sensor ‘Interference’ After $35K Polymarket Payouts
items.desc: Long-shot bets on a market tied to the daily maximum temperature in Paris have raised eyebrows—and triggered a police complaint.

items.title: JPMorgan says DeFi exploits and stagnant TVL continue to limit institutional appeal
items.desc: JPMorgan said persistent hacks appear to push investors toward Tether's USDT, as users move funds out of DeFi during stress.

items.title: Tether freezes $344 million in USDT on Tron tied to 'illicit activity'
items.desc: The stablecoin issuer said the action followed U.S. law enforcement requests as global watchdog FATF warned of growing role of digital dollars in illicit money flows.

items.title: Tether Freezes $344 Million in USDT Stablecoins Flagged for Illicit Activity
items.desc: Stablecoin issuer Tether said it coordinated with U.S. authorities to implement one of its largest asset freezes on record.

items.title: Tether Freezes $344M USDT in Coordination with U.S. Law Enforcement
items.desc: The stablecoin issuer says it has now supported more than 2,300 cases worldwide, including over 1,200 with U.S. agencies.

items.title: JPMorgan says persistent security flaws curb DeFi’s institutional appeal
items.desc: A $20 billion hit from the KelpDAO exploit highlights systemic risks, while flat ETH-denominated growth and a shift to stablecoins point to ongoing fragility in DeFi.

items.title: BIS warns cryptocurrency exchanges are becoming ‘shadow banks,’ and why that's a risk
items.desc: The Bank for International Settlements (BIS) released a report warning stablecoin yields and other DeFi “earn” products are bank-like services without the safeguards or insurance.

items.title: FTX estate misses out on $3B Cursor stake value after $200K sale in 2023
items.desc: The FTX estate sold its Cursor stake for $200K in 2023, now worth $3B after a SpaceX-linked valuation surge, raising questions about bankruptcy asset sales.

items.title: 100+ Crypto Firms Urge Senate to Advance Clarity Act, Warn of Innovation Moving Offshore
items.desc: Bitcoin Magazine 100+ Crypto Firms Urge Senate to Advance Clarity Act, Warn of Innovation Moving Offshore Over 100 cryptocurrency firms and industry organizations are urging the U.S. Senate to move forward on long-delayed market structure legislation, cautioning that further delays could drive innovation and capital abroad. This post 100+ Crypto Firms Urge Senate to Advance Clarity Act, Warn of Innovation Moving Offshore first appeared on Bitcoin Magazine and is written by Micah Zimmerman.

items.title: 'Excellent Window' for Strategic Accumulation as Bitcoin Risk Indicator Flips Bullish
items.desc: With Bitcoin topping $79K Wednesday, a "cleared risk landscape" hints at a potential extension of its ongoing rally.

items.title: Lotus Taps WisdomTree Money Market Fund to Build Yield Floor into DeFi Lending
items.desc: WisdomTree's WTGXX holds nearly $860M in distributed asset value, mostly on Ethereum.

items.title: U.S. military runs Bitcoin node, sees crypto as power projection versus China
items.desc: Admiral Samuel Paparo, head of US Indo-Pacific Command, told two congressional panels this week that the military is running a live Bitcoin node for cybersecurity testing and views the protocol as a tool of national power in competition with China.

items.title: Sam Bankman-Fried withdraws retrial motion. He believes he would not get a fair trial.
items.desc: The founder of the collapsed crypto exchange FTX said he might file for a retrial after a decision on his appeal is ruled upon.

items.title: Blockchain Capital Seeks $700M for Two New Crypto Funds
items.desc: The venture capital firm aims to close the fundraising within five to six months, and is already deploying some of the new capital.

items.title: US Government Runs a Bitcoin Node, But Not Mining BTC: US Admiral
items.desc: The commander of U.S. forces in the Pacific said the military is investigating Bitcoin’s ability to “secure and protect networks.”

items.title: Circle Proposes Emergency Rate Changes to Unstick Aave's Frozen USDC Pool
items.desc: Stablecoin issuer argues Aave's interest rate curve is failing to clear the $1.89B pool after four days at full utilization.

items.title: FBI Director Kash Patel To Speak at Bitcoin 2026 Conference About “Ending the War on Bitcoin”
items.desc: Bitcoin Magazine FBI Director Kash Patel To Speak at Bitcoin 2026 Conference About “Ending the War on Bitcoin” A panel titled “Code Is Free Speech: Ending The War On Bitcoin” has been announced for Bitcoin 2026, bringing together three figures from law, government, and the Bitcoin industry. The panel features FBI Director Kash Patel, Deputy Attorney General Todd Blanche, and Coinbase Chief Legal Officer Paul Grewal. Kash Patel was confirmed as FBI Director […] This post FBI Director Kash Patel To Speak at Bitcoin 2026 Conference About “Ending the War on Bitcoin” first appeared on Bitcoin Magazine and is written by Jenna Montgomery.

items.title: The U.S. Military Is Running a Bitcoin Node, Admiral Paparo Reveals
items.desc: Bitcoin Magazine The U.S. Military Is Running a Bitcoin Node, Admiral Paparo Reveals The U.S. military is actively running a Bitcoin node and testing the protocol’s cryptographic architecture as a tool for securing and protecting networks, according to INDOPACOM commander Admiral Samuel Paparo. This post The U.S. Military Is Running a Bitcoin Node, Admiral Paparo Reveals first appeared on Bitcoin Magazine and is written by Micah Zimmerman.

items.title: “Bitcoin as Everyday Money” Event to Rally Industry Behind De Minimis Tax Framework at Bitcoin 2026
items.desc: Bitcoin Magazine “Bitcoin as Everyday Money” Event to Rally Industry Behind De Minimis Tax Framework at Bitcoin 2026 Policy leaders from Block and the Bitcoin Policy Institute will headline a live event at Bitcoin 2026 calling for de minimis tax relief that treats qualifying network digital assets like Bitcoin as cash-like for small transactions, responding directly to proposals that would limit relief to stablecoins only. This post “Bitcoin as Everyday Money” Event to Rally Industry Behind De Minimis Tax Framework at Bitcoin 2026 first appeared on Bitcoin Magazine and is written by Juan Galt.

items.title: U.S. Treasury Secretary Presses Senate to Pass Crypto Market Structure Legislation
items.desc: Bitcoin Magazine U.S. Treasury Secretary Presses Senate to Pass Crypto Market Structure Legislation Treasury Secretary Scott Bessent told a Senate panel Wednesday that passing comprehensive crypto legislation is vital to maintaining U.S. financial leadership. This post U.S. Treasury Secretary Presses Senate to Pass Crypto Market Structure Legislation first appeared on Bitcoin Magazine and is written by Micah Zimmerman.

items.title: SparkLend Sees Over $1B in Deposits Since Kelp Exploit as Aave TVL Plunges
items.desc: Aave TVL has dropped by $10B since the Kelp attackers used the protocol to borrow $190 million in WETH, depositing unbacked rsETH.

items.title: Base Pushes Toward Final L2 Stage with First Independent Upgrade
items.desc: Base's Azul upgrade is currently live on testnet, with mainnet launch scheduled for mid-May.

items.title: Russia’s Sberbank Ready to Enter Crypto Trading as Russia Moves Toward Regulation
items.desc: Bitcoin Magazine Russia’s Sberbank Ready to Enter Crypto Trading as Russia Moves Toward Regulation Sberbank will provide access to crypto trading once regulation and organized exchange trading begin, Senior Vice President Ruslan Vesterovsky said. This post Russia’s Sberbank Ready to Enter Crypto Trading as Russia Moves Toward Regulation first appeared on Bitcoin Magazine and is written by Micah Zimmerman.

items.title: American Bitcoin ($ABTC) Activates 11,000 New Bitcoin Miners, Expanding Hashrate Capacity
items.desc: Bitcoin Magazine American Bitcoin ($ABTC) Activates 11,000 New Bitcoin Miners, Expanding Hashrate Capacity American Bitcoin (ABTC) announced it had energized more than 11,000 new mining machines at its Drumheller facility. This post American Bitcoin ($ABTC) Activates 11,000 New Bitcoin Miners, Expanding Hashrate Capacity first appeared on Bitcoin Magazine and is written by Micah Zimmerman.

items.title: Bitcoin Core 31.0 released
items.desc: Bitcoin Core version 31.0 is now available for download. See the release notes for more information about the bug fixes in this release. If you have any questions, please stop by the #bitcoin IRC chatroom (IRC, web) and we’ll do our best to help you.

items.title: Bitcoin Core 31.0 released
items.desc: Bitcoin Core version 31.0 is now available for download. See the release notes for more information about the bug fixes in this release. If you have any questions, please stop by the #bitcoin IRC chatroom (IRC, web) and we’ll do our best to help you.
